name: Gokshura
usesInAyurveda: Tridoshic Modulation: Powerful Vata and Pitta Hara (pacifies Vata and Pitta doshas). Due to its Madhura (sweet) Rasa and Sheeta (cooling) Virya, it is the most prestigious herb for 'Mutra-janana' (diuretic) and 'Vajikarana' (aphrodisiac) therapies, clearing the fiery and agitated qualities of Pitta and nourishing the dry Vata. It may slightly increase Kapha if used in excess.
Karma (Actions): Mutra-janana (diuretic), Vajikarana (aphrodisiac), Shulaghna (analgesic), Ashmarighna (stone-breaker), Brinhaniya (nourishing), and Hridya (heart-tonic).

API/AFI Adherence: The dried whole plant and fruit are the official medicinal parts as per the Ayurvedic Pharmacopoeia of India (API). It is a primary ingredient in iconic classical formulations like Dashamoola-arishta, Gokshuradi Guggulu, and various urinary tonics.
Specific Indications: Used in Mutra-kricchra (burning urination), Ashmari (kidney stones), Klaibya (impotence), Shotha (generalized edema), and Katishoola (low back pain).
usageForm: Fruit powder (Churna), Whole plant decoction (Kwatha), Medicated ghee (Ghrita), Medicated oil (Taila).
mainMedicinalUses: Urinary and Kidney Care Specialist: Gokshura is the premier Ayurvedic remedy for all conditions of the urinary system. Its Sheeta (cooling) and Mutra-janana (diuretic) nature helps in relieving 'Mutra-kricchra' (burning urination) and managing urinary tract infections. WHO-aligned research supports its antilitiic (stone-clearing) and diuretic effects.

Vitality and Reproductive Health: Historically known as 'Vajikarana' (aphrodisiac). It effectively improves sperm count, libido, and sexual stamina by promoting the healthy production of 'Shukra-dhatu' (reproductive tissue) and providing systemic nourishing (Brimhana) action.

Kidney Stone Breaker: Its 'Ashmarighna' (stone-breaking) and 'Tikshna' (sharp) properties make it incomparable for managing kidney stones by clearing blocked urinary pathways and preventing the formation of crystals in the bladder through its cooling-diuretic action.

Heart and Cardiovascular Health: Acts as a mild Hridya (heart-tonic). It helps in regulating blood pressure (Hridya-rogahara) and protecting the heart muscles from oxidative stress through its potent antioxidant profile and Vata-Pitta balancing action.

Muscle Strength and Athletics: Widely used for its ability to improve muscle tone and physical stamina. It is traditionally used to promote healthy growth and recovery from fatigue in athletes through its 'Rasayana' (rejuvenative) quality.

Gastrointestinal Relief: Its 'Deepana' (appetizer) and 'Pachana' (digestive) property help in clearing 'Ama' (toxins) from the gut and managing inflammatory conditions of the digestive lining through its cooling-astringent properties.

Phytochemical Profile: Rich in Steroidal Saponins (Protodioscin, Terrestrosin), Harmine (alkaloid), and Flavonoids that provide its multifaceted medicinal benefits, particularly its potent testosterone-boosting and anti-inflammatory properties.
plantProfile: Unique History: Classified under the 'Dashamoola' (ten roots) and 'Balya' (strength) groups in ancient Ayurvedic treatises like Charaka Samhita. It is spiritually associated with 'Sattva' (purity and clarity). Mentioned in classical Sanskrit literature as 'Sthala-padma' (the auspicious-flower of the land).

Physical Characteristics: A prostrate, spreading annual herb growing along the ground, with many branches and attractive small yellow flowers arranged in terminal spikes. It features pinnate leaves with 5-8 pairs of leaflets and distinctive, spiny fruit (capsule) that resembles a cow's hoof (Gokshura). It is found throughout India and tropical/sub-tropical regions worldwide as a common weed.

Specific Chemical Compounds: Primarily contains Saponins (Protodioscin), Flavonoids (Kaempferol), and Alkaloids (Harmine). The presence of protodioscin is crucial for its bio-enhancing, anti-microbial, and hormone-libido increasing action on the immune system.

Strict Standardizations: The fruit is standardized in API based on macroscopic features (spiny, hard spheres). Physicochemical benchmarks: total ash (<15%), alcohol-soluble extractive (>6%), and water-soluble extractive (>10%). HPTLC quantification is used to monitor protodioscin and saponin content.
preparationMethods: Fruit Powder (Churna): 3-6g of fine powder taken twice daily with warm milk or honey (for libido) or warm water (for general vitality).
Whole Plant Decoction (Kwatha): 20g of dried plant boiled in 160ml water reduced to 40ml for chronic urinary issues and fevers. Take twice daily before meals.
Gokshura Ghrita: 5-10g of ghee processed with Gokshura; taken to treat chronic mental stress and low reproductive health through its 'Rasayana' quality.
Medicated Milk (Ksheerapaka): 5g of powder boiled in 200ml milk; taken to support kidney health and manage cardiac issues.
External Paste: Ground fresh leaves or bark apply directly to localized inflammations and skin rashes to reduce heat and swelling.

dosages.Children: Generally safe. 125 mg to 250 mg of powder mixed with honey twice daily for respiratory and digestive protection during childhood infections.
dosages.Adults: Powder: 3-6 grams; Decoction: 50-100 ml; Ghee: 5-10 g, divided throughout the day before or after meals.
dosages.Elderly: Ideal for age-related Vata issues and maintaining cardiac health. Dosage: 3g of powder daily with warm milk at bedtime.

safetyProfile: Contraindications: Generally very safe. However, individuals with high Kapha (cold, excess mucus) should use it in moderation as it is Guru (heavy). Avoid in extreme constipation due to its 'Grahi' (absorbent) nature.
GRAS Status: Classified as a safe medicinal herb with a history of use spanning millennia across various traditional systems (Ayurveda, Unani, and TCM) without documented toxicity.
Herb-Drug Interactions: Monitor if taken alongside synthetic anti-diuretic or sedative medications as it may potentiate their effects. No severe adverse interactions reported in WHO monographs.
Adverse Effects: None documented at traditional therapeutic levels. Excessive use in very sensitive individuals may rarely cause mild skin cooling or stomach heaviness.
